title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what motivates people to take part in extreme sports and what the risks and rewards of these activities might be.

images.label: A
images.description: A skydiver in freefall, arms spread wide against a backdrop of clouds and patchwork fields thousands of metres below, looking exhilarated

images.label: B
images.description: A rock climber without a rope soloing a sheer vertical cliff face, fingers gripping a tiny hold with intense concentration

followUp.question: Do you think people have the right to take dangerous risks for personal enjoyment, even if it means others may have to rescue them?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what drives people to push their personal limits and what they might gain from these experiences.

images.label: D
images.description: A marathon runner crossing the finish line in a city race, completely exhausted but with tears of joy and overwhelming relief on their face

images.label: E
images.description: A person completing their first public speech, visibly nervous but standing tall at a lectern in front of a large audience

followUp.question: Do you think overcoming personal challenges and taking risks is essential for personal growth? Can you think of examples?
